body:
For a typical residential garage door, a professional can usually replace the springs in **about 1–2 hours**. In many straightforward cases, the actual spring replacement takes **45–90 minutes** , with the remaining time spent inspecting, balancing, and testing the door to ensure it operates safely. [Garage Door Spring Repair: A Complete Guide to Signs, Costs, and Safety | OGD®](https://ogd.com/garage-door-spring-repair-guide/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[How Long to Replace a Garage Door Spring? (Pro vs DIY) | HowLongFor.com](https://howlongfor.com/home/replace-a-garage-door-spring?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

The total time can be longer if:

  * The door has damaged cables, rollers, or drums that also need replacement.

  * The garage door uses an uncommon spring size that isn't immediately available.

  * It's a large or custom garage door.

  * Both springs are being replaced (which is often recommended if one has already failed because they typically wear out at a similar rate). [Garage Door Spring Repair: A Complete Guide to Signs, Costs, and Safety | OGD®](https://ogd.com/garage-door-spring-repair-guide/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[How Long Does Garage Door Spring Repair Take? (Cost + Time)](https://fixurge.com/blog/how-long-does-garage-door-spring-repair-take/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

One important safety note: **torsion springs are under very high tension** , so replacing them is generally considered a job for a trained technician rather than a DIY project. [Garage Door Spring Repair: A Complete Guide to Signs, Costs, and Safety | OGD®](https://ogd.com/garage-door-spring-repair-guide/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[How Long Does Garage Door Spring Repair Take? (Cost + Time)](https://fixurge.com/blog/how-long-does-garage-door-spring-repair-take/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If your spring has already broken, I can also give you a typical **cost estimate** based on your location.
